name: Enterprise Theory Cross-Check Matrix
description: Cross-checks established enterprise software-engineering and governance theory against how real benchmark systems (open-source ERP/CRM/CMS/e-commerce and reference architectures) represent each concept, and against how LocalAIFactory currently models it. Each row records honest gaps and a concrete proof needed to claim the concept is fully supported. Evidence and proofs are framed at confidence levels; no vendor certification is asserted.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Bounded contexts, aggregates, entities, value objects, and a ubiquitous language; ERP suites split into modules that act as bounded contexts with their own models.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Open-source ERP/CRM separate accounting, inventory, and CRM into distinct module models; rich domain objects own invariants.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Core project holds domain entities, enums, and abstractions with no framework dependencies; Data/Rag/Agent/Ingestion form context-like boundaries (e.g. knowledge, retrieval, inference).
concepts.gaps: Boundaries are largely technical layers, not explicitly named bounded contexts; aggregates and invariants are not formally documented as a ubiquitous language.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Document bounded contexts and aggregate roots with invariants, and show an import of a real banking domain mapped to them.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Concentric dependency rule: domain at the center, frameworks/IO at the edges; dependencies point inward.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Reference .NET solutions split Domain/Application/Infrastructure/Web with inward dependencies and no domain reference to the web framework.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Eight projects with no dependency cycles; Core references nothing; Web is the composition root; verified by the documented project graph.
concepts.gaps: Application-layer use-case objects are not always explicit; some orchestration lives in controllers/services rather than dedicated use-case handlers.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Add an explicit application/use-case layer and a dependency test that fails the build on any inward-rule violation.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Core logic exposes ports (interfaces); adapters implement them for DB, web, messaging, and external services, enabling swap-in/out.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Pluggable storage/inference adapters in retrieval frameworks; vendor-agnostic interfaces for vector stores and model runtimes.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Abstractions in Core (e.g. health cache, retrieval, model execution) with Qdrant/Ollama as optional adapters that degrade gracefully when absent.
concepts.gaps: Adapter surface is informal; no formal port catalog; some adapters assume a single implementation.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Publish a port/adapter catalog and demonstrate two interchangeable adapters for one port (e.g. an alternate vector store).

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Separate read and write models; event-sourced systems persist a log of domain events and rebuild state by replay.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: High-volume systems use read replicas/materialized views; some use event stores for audit and projections.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Read/write separation is partial: list views use lightweight read-model records (a documented rule); writes go through services. No event sourcing.
concepts.gaps: No command/query bus, no event store; the read-model pattern is a performance convention, not a formal CQRS implementation.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Introduce explicit command/query handlers for one workflow and, if needed, an append-only event log with projection replay.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Business processes modeled as tasks, gateways, and events; workflow engines execute and track state and approvals.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: ERP/ITSM tools embed approval workflows (purchase, change, leave) with state machines and audit trails.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: An approval lifecycle for knowledge/agent proposals with explicit gates and an audit trail; documented approval workflow.
concepts.gaps: No general BPMN engine; workflows are coded, not modeled/configurable; limited to the knowledge/agent approval domain.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Express the approval lifecycle as an explicit state machine with a diagram and show a second workflow reusing the engine.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: General ledger, AP/AR, inventory, procurement, HR, and reporting integrated around a shared chart of accounts.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Open-source ERPs (Odoo, ERPNext) implement these as integrated modules with shared masters and posting rules.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Not an ERP; the professional-base pack carries accounting/finance/banking awareness knowledge, and integration patterns target Odoo/ERPNext.
concepts.gaps: No native ledger or ERP modules; relationship is integration/advisory, not implementation.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Demonstrate importing and reasoning over a real ERP schema and producing correct module-aware guidance.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Lead-to-cash: leads, opportunities, accounts, activities, and pipeline stages with conversion metrics.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: CRM modules model stage transitions, ownership, and activity history with reporting.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Not a CRM; awareness-level knowledge only; integration patterns can read CRM data via APIs.
concepts.gaps: No CRM entities or pipeline modeling natively.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Show end-to-end reasoning over an imported CRM pipeline with correct stage/lifecycle interpretation.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Incident, problem, and change records with priorities, SLAs, approvals, and CMDB linkage.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: ITSM tools track incident lifecycle, change advisory approvals, and SLA timers.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: The support-issue learning registry plus runbooks (backup/restore, rollback, autonomous-engineering) capture incident patterns and change-with-approval; no SLA timers.
concepts.gaps: No formal SLA tracking, CMDB, or incident ticket lifecycle; change approval is for engineering proposals, not a general CAB.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Add SLA timing and an incident lifecycle to the support registry and link incidents to affected components.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: SLOs/SLIs, error budgets, graceful degradation, and blameless postmortems.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Reliability-focused services define SLIs (latency, availability) and degrade non-critical features under failure.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Strong graceful degradation (MSSQL-only mode; optional Qdrant/Ollama), cached health snapshots, and a sub-second page-latency target with request timing logs.
concepts.gaps: No formal SLOs/error budgets; latency target is a convention, not a tracked SLI with alerting.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Define explicit SLOs/SLIs and capture them in the benchmark/health telemetry with breach detection.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Repeatable CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code, automated release and rollback.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Pipelines build, test, package, and deploy with retained artifacts and rollback paths.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Scripted build/run/migrate, packaging and release docs, deployment-drill scripts, and an IIS deployment runbook; firsthand deploy issues captured in the registry.
concepts.gaps: Release automation is script-based rather than a full CI/CD pipeline; rollback is documented but partly manual.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Run an automated deploy-then-rollback drill and capture artifacts/evidence end to end.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: A tiered verification standard for application security controls (auth, session, access control, validation, logging).
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Security-reviewed apps map features to ASVS requirements at a target level.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Windows/Negotiate auth proven at IIS, least-privilege SQL (is_sysadmin=0), append-only audit, no secrets in repo (Data Protection), 0-HIGH security audit; mapped in docs/security/OWASP-ASVS-Mapping.md.
concepts.gaps: No external pen-test, self-signed TLS in pilot, app-level auth is dev-grade behind IIS, no formal ASVS-level certification.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Complete a targeted ASVS-level self-assessment and an independent security test against the mapping.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Secure software development practices grouped into prepare-the-organization, protect-the-software, produce-well-secured-software, respond-to-vulnerabilities.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Mature projects evidence secure build, dependency management, and vulnerability response.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Documented architecture rules, no-secrets policy, additive-migration discipline, security audit, and a fix-loop/runbook for response; mapped in docs/security/NIST-SSDF-Mapping.md.
concepts.gaps: No formal vulnerability-disclosure process, no automated dependency scanning evidence, partial threat modeling.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Add dependency-scan evidence, a documented vuln-response SLA, and a recorded threat model.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Govern/Map/Measure/Manage functions for trustworthy AI: provenance, human oversight, and risk controls.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Governed AI systems record model/version, human-in-the-loop approval, and output provenance.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Approval lifecycle for AI outputs, output provenance/approval docs, AI governance doc, and human-in-the-loop gates; local-first reduces data-exfiltration risk.
concepts.gaps: No quantitative model-risk measurement, no bias/eval harness evidence, oversight is procedural not metricized.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Add measurable AI evaluation (accuracy/safety) tied to the approval gate and a model-risk register.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Security woven through requirements, design, build, test, and release with gates at each stage.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Projects show design review, secure-coding standards, and pre-release security gates.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: CLAUDE.md as a contract, build+runtime validation gates, security audit before release, and approval gates for changes.
concepts.gaps: No formal design-phase threat modeling artifact; security gate is audit-based rather than continuous.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Introduce a recorded threat-modeling step and continuous security checks in the build gate.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Ownership, classification, lineage, retention, and access policy over data assets.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Enterprise data platforms track lineage and classification with retention rules.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Curated knowledge with an approval lifecycle, provenance retention, data-protection plan, and least-privilege access; MSSQL as the system of record.
concepts.gaps: No formal data-classification taxonomy or automated lineage across all stores; retention is policy-described not enforced everywhere.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Add a classification taxonomy and demonstrate lineage from import through approval to retrieval.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Tamper-evident, append-only audit trails linking who did what, when, and on what version.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Regulated systems keep immutable audit logs and provenance for derived outputs.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Append-only audit model, AI output provenance/approval, and an audit doc; knowledge items carry source attribution and review status.
concepts.gaps: Append-only is enforced by design/policy; no cryptographic tamper-evidence (hash chaining) demonstrated.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Add hash-chained audit entries and verify tamper-evidence with a recorded check.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Horizontal scale, stateless app tier, async I/O, and read scaling under high concurrency.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Scalable apps keep the app tier stateless and push state to caches/DB; async throughout.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Async controllers, scope-per-task parallel reads, no blocking external calls on the request path, lightweight read models, sub-second page target.
concepts.gaps: Single-node by design (local-first); no horizontal-scale or load-test evidence at high concurrency.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Run a concurrency/load benchmark and record latency/throughput under realistic load.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Cache-aside/read-through with explicit invalidation and TTLs to avoid stale reads.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Services cache hot reads and invalidate on write or by TTL.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Cached service-health snapshot (IServiceHealthCache) refreshed by a background timer instead of on-request probes; a deliberate, documented pattern.
concepts.gaps: Caching is focused on health; no general application-data cache or formal invalidation strategy beyond TTL refresh.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Define cache keys/TTLs/invalidation for one hot data path and measure hit ratio and staleness bounds.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Versioned, forward-only-or-reversible schema changes applied automatically and tracked.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Frameworks apply ordered migrations and track applied versions; prefer additive changes.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: EF Core migrations auto-applied on startup, additive-by-default rule, no destructive changes without approval, snapshot kept EF-generated; 34-table schema.
concepts.gaps: Down-migrations/rollback for destructive changes rely on backups rather than reversible scripts.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Demonstrate a reversible migration with a verified up/down and a backup-based recovery drill.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Role-based and attribute-based access control governing who can perform which action on which resource.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: ERP/CMS enforce roles and fine-grained permissions; ABAC adds attribute conditions.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Windows-identity auth at IIS, least-privilege SQL roles, claims/roles mapping docs, approval-gated actions; access decisions are role-oriented.
concepts.gaps: No fine-grained ABAC policy engine; app-level authorization is coarse and tied to Windows identity behind IIS.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Implement an explicit role/permission matrix and, if needed, attribute-based conditions with tests.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Consistent resource modeling, versioning, auth, pagination, error contracts, and idempotency.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Mature REST/GraphQL APIs version endpoints, paginate, and define stable error shapes.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: MVC controllers with clear request/response handling; integration knowledge for consuming WordPress/Odoo/Frappe/Woo/Magento/Drupal APIs; no large public API surface of its own.
concepts.gaps: Internal-first; no formally versioned public API, OpenAPI contract, or idempotency guarantees documented.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Publish an OpenAPI contract with versioning, pagination, and error/idempotency conventions for any external API.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Structured logs, metrics, and traces with correlation to diagnose production behavior.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Services emit structured logs and request traces with timing and correlation IDs.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: RequestTimingMiddleware logs per-request start/complete with duration (warning > 1s) and dashboard build timing; a documented hang-detection technique.
concepts.gaps: No distributed tracing or metrics backend; observability is log-based and single-node.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Add structured metrics/traces with correlation IDs and a queryable telemetry store.

concepts.howRealSystemsRepresentIt: Timeouts, fallbacks, and feature degradation so a dependency failure does not take down the system.
concepts.evidenceFromBenchmarkSystems: Resilient apps fall back when optional services are down and never block core pages on them.
concepts.howLocalAIFactoryModelsIt: Core pages must always render on MSSQL-only; Qdrant/Ollama optional and gated; health read from cache; a hard project rule.
concepts.gaps: No formal circuit-breaker/retry-policy library; degradation is rule-enforced rather than centrally managed.
concepts.proofToAdvance: Add explicit timeout/retry/circuit-breaker policies and prove core pages render with every optional service down.
